add_params.name: ID
add_params.description: ID of the IntuneReusableSettingTemplate to deploy; sent as 'TemplateId' in older path. PS1 reads $Request.Body.TemplateId ?? $Request.Body.TemplateList?.value ?? $Request.Body.TemplateList. The edit form sends 'ID' and 'TemplateId' both set to the same value.

add_params.name: TemplateId
add_params.description: Legacy alias for ID; PS1 resolves TemplateId first then falls back to ID via TemplateList.

add_params.name: displayName
add_params.description: Display name to override; propagated into rawJSON by formatter.

add_params.name: rawJSON
add_params.description: Serialised JSON string of the full reusable-policy-setting Graph object. PS1 reads this from the template entity, not directly from the request body — the body only supplies TemplateId.

notes: The edit-form customDataformatter sends: tenantFilter, ID, TemplateId (=ID), displayName, description, rawJSON. However, the PS1 does NOT read rawJSON from the request — it looks up the template by TemplateId from Azure Table Storage and uses the stored RawJSON. The body fields 'displayName' and 'description' are not used by the PS1 either; it reads them from the stored template JSON. Effectively the API only needs tenantFilter + TemplateId to function. The other body fields are ignored server-side. Marking as verified with that caveat noted.
